tool.name: GaCDI Manifest Downloader
tool.description: Download files listed in a GDC or PDC manifest

param.name: manifest_file
param.label: Manifest file (GDC or PDC — the commons is auto-detected)

param.name: extract_archives
param.label: Extract archives after download
param.help: Unpack recognized archives (.tar.gz, .tgz, .tar.bz2, .tar.xz, .tar, .zip, .gz, .bz2, .xz) in place, keeping only the extracted contents in the output collection. The archive itself moves aside rather than being deleted; it only reappears in the output if extraction fails.

tool.help:
**What it does**
Downloads the files listed in a manifest exported from either the NCI `Genomic Data Commons (GDC) <https://portal.gdc.cancer.gov/>`_ or the `Proteomic Data Commons (PDC) <https://pdc.cancer.gov/>`_, using ``mcdi download``. The manifest's data commons is auto-detected from its header row, so the same tool handles either kind of manifest without any extra configuration.
**Manifest**
- **GDC**: build a file cart in the `GDC portal <https://portal.gdc.cancer.gov/>`_ and download the manifest (TSV), or generate one via the API (``GET https://api.gdc.cancer.gov/v0/manifest/<uuid1>,<uuid2>,...``). - **PDC**: on the `PDC portal <https://pdc.cancer.gov/>`_ Explore page, filter to the files you want and use "Export File Manifest" (CSV or TSV). PDC manifests embed signed download links that expire after 7 days — re-export if a run starts failing on files that previously worked.
**Authorization token (optional, GDC only)**
A GDC authorization token is only required to download **controlled-access** GDC datasets; open-access GDC data, and all PDC data, download without one. Tokens are downloaded from the `GDC Data Portal <https://portal.gdc.cancer.gov/>`_, under your account profile, as a text file (e.g. ``gdc-user-token.txt``).
To use your authorization token, store it using Galaxy's credentials system, which keeps it encrypted and injects it securely at runtime — paste the *contents* of the downloaded token file as the secret value. See `Galaxy's credentials documentation <https://galaxyproject.org/news/2026-01-12-tool-credentials-system/#how-to-use-it-for-galaxy-users>`_ for instructions on adding your GDC token as a credential.
**Access check before downloading**
Every file in the manifest is checked for accessibility before any download starts. If even one file is inaccessible (e.g. a controlled-access GDC file without a valid token), the whole run fails immediately, naming the file and why — instead of downloading most of a large manifest only to fail on the last file. A few failed files also get a couple of automatic retries with a short wait in between, since a retried Galaxy job starts from an empty working directory rather than resuming a failed one.
**Extract archives after download**
Some commons files are themselves archives (e.g. a ``.tar.gz`` bundle of slides). Turning this on unpacks any recognized archive (``.tar.gz``, ``.tgz``, ``.tar.bz2``, ``.tar.xz``, ``.tar``, ``.zip``, ``.gz``, ``.bz2``, ``.xz``) right where it was downloaded, and the output collection then holds only the extracted contents — not a redundant copy of the packed archive alongside them. The archive itself isn't deleted, just moved out of the way; if extraction fails for a given file, that archive is left in place instead, so there's still something in the collection for it. Off by default.
**Output**
A collection containing the files listed in the manifest, as downloaded from the corresponding data commons — or, for any file that was extracted, its extracted contents in place of the packed archive.
